context: FOMC opens today. Kevin Warsh chairs his first Fed meeting (sworn in May 22, replacing Powell). Rate hold at 3.50–3.75% is 98% priced — the market isn't trading the decision, it's trading the dot-plot tomorrow at 14:00 ET. Three scenarios: (A) Neutral/fewer-cuts = range holds, BTC $66K–$67,500; (B) Warsh kills the dot-plot = unambiguously bullish — removes forward guidance friction, crypto prices higher uncertainty premium out; (C) Hawkish dots = BTC re-tests $64,200, XRP flushes to $1.18, SUI sweeps $0.745. Fear & Greed at 23 (Extreme Fear) while BTC is +5% on the week — sentiment is lagging price, a coiled-spring dynamic. CME open interest washed from $42B to $25B since May = deleveraged clean slate, perp put-OI > call-OI = institutional hedges in place but net positioning is SHORT into the catalyst. Historically, a clean FOMC hold with neutral framing resolves this divergence upward. XRP is the standout independent driver: $1.44B cumulative ETF inflows, 6-week streak, whale accumulation aligned — CLARITY Act on Senate calendar with ~8 weeks before summer recess. Coinbase 'Everything Exchange' announcement today adds a wildcard. SUI -2% today, range-bound; HBAR flat again.

tickers.thesis: FOMC eve: BTC flat +0.36% today, holding the two-week high at $66K. Rate hold at 3.50-3.75% is 98% priced (CME FedWatch). What the market trades is Warsh's dot-plot Wednesday — neutral/fewer-cuts = stay range; hawkish dots = $64K test; kill-the-dot-plot = unambiguously bullish (removes forward guidance friction). Coinbase premium turned positive for the first time in weeks on June 13, signalling US institutional demand warming. CME OI washed out from $42B to $25B since May = deleveraged clean slate into FOMC. Don't pre-position today; await the 14:00 ET decision and 14:30 Warsh presser as the real trigger. No new long until a post-FOMC re-test of $65,500 holds or a clean break above $67,500 on the dovish/neutral scenario.

insights.title: Sentiment easing: F&G 23 (Extreme Fear), Δ +3 vs yesterday
insights.body: Crypto Fear & Greed Index printed 23 (Extreme Fear) at 05:29Z — +3 vs yesterday's reading. F&G under 30 historically aligns with bounce-watch zones; under 20 with capitulation. Combined with the recent price action and live headlines (see LIVE FEED), the regime is risk-off until a sweep-and-reclaim candle prints. No new longs until F&G stops falling AND BTC reclaims its 24h high.

insights.title: Cross-exchange divergence on XRP: 0.33%
insights.body: Multi-source price triangulation (CoinGecko + Binance + Bybit) shows XRP divergence of 0.33% at 05:29Z. Divergence >0.5% across major venues is a thin-liquidity / arb-window signal. At 0.33% it's still in normal range but worth monitoring — widening = exit signal for sized trades on XRP.

insights.title: FOMC dot-plot is the week's only trade
insights.body: Rate hold is 99% priced — BTC barely moves on the decision itself. What moves crypto is the dot-plot (SEP) and Warsh's tone at 14:30 ET Wednesday. Warsh is structurally hawkish vs Powell. If he signals fewer 2026 cuts or neutral-to-restrictive bias, BTC tests $64,200 and the rest follow. If he scraps the dot-plot, that's a net-positive because forward guidance uncertainty is removed. No action today; size after the presser.

insights.title: CME OI washout: coiled spring into FOMC
insights.body: Bitcoin CME open interest collapsed from $42B in early May to $25B now — a six-month low. The deleveraging is complete. Perp put-OI exceeds call-OI, meaning institutional hedges are already in place. A clean hold + neutral Warsh language turns those hedges into fuel for a squeeze. CME also launched Bitcoin Volatility futures June 5 — institutional participants now have a new product anchoring them to BTC vol, adding structural baseline demand.

insights.title: XRP: dual structural bid independent of FOMC
insights.body: XRP ETF cumulative inflows hit $1.44B with 6 consecutive weeks of net inflows — the longest streak in 2026. On-chain whales bought 190M XRP in one week while exchange reserves hit a 2026 low. Both signals converging (whale + ETF aligned) for the first time this cycle. CLARITY Act is on the Senate floor calendar — leadership scheduling announcement is the next trigger, not FOMC. XRP pullback to $1.18–$1.20 post-FOMC is the highest-conviction entry this week regardless of Warsh's tone.

insights.title: SUI: $65B stablecoin volume not priced in
insights.body: Since June 10 Sui activated protocol-level gasless stablecoin transfers; $65B in volume followed in 5 days. That's comparable to entire L1 TVL claims from competitors. T. Rowe Price active crypto ETF approved June 12 with SUI included — first major AM diversified exposure to SUI specifically. Price is -2% today and at the week low of $0.778, which means the adoption story hasn't been priced. If FOMC resolves neutral and $0.745 holds, SUI is the altcoin retrace to buy.

insights.title: HBAR: structural laggard — OKCoin Japan is real but not enough alone
insights.body: HBAR OKCoin Japan yen pair listing opens Japanese retail for the first time. Canary ETF holds 549M HBAR. SEC-CFTC digital commodity classification confirmed. But price is +0.76% on the week versus XRP +5% and SUI +5% — HBAR is structurally a laggard in this cycle. No chase below $0.088. The OTE entry is $0.075–$0.078 on a FOMC flush; the breakout trigger is $0.103. Everything between is noise.

insights.title: F&G 23 + BTC +5% 7d = sentiment/price divergence → resolves UP post-catalyst
insights.body: Fear & Greed at 23 (Extreme Fear) is a mis-match with BTC's 7-day +5% performance. The market is fearful despite the price not reflecting fear. Historically when F&G lags price by more than 10 points heading into a known catalyst, it resolves with price pulling sentiment up (not the other way). The coil fires upward if FOMC is not-hawkish. The risk is F&G was right all along and FOMC delivers the flush.

hidden_signals.body: Fear & Greed at 23 (Extreme Fear), Δ +3 vs yesterday — sub-30 zones historically align with bounce setups but require a sweep-and-reclaim candle to confirm

Warsh may scrap the dot-plot at this meeting (Jun 17). Removing the Summary of Economic Projections eliminates a major forward-guidance mechanism. Implication: markets lose a key hawkish signalling tool — net crypto-positive because terminal-rate uncertainty is removed and risk assets re-price without the Fed's 'upper dot' anchoring rate expectations.

Coinbase 'Everything Exchange' announcement today (Jun 16). Scope unknown as of this write, but Coinbase teased a product bundling exchange, custody, staking, and derivatives in one platform. If it includes spot XRP or SUI margin products, flow implication is immediate. Watch the 9:30 AM ET event for any crypto-on-ramp expansion.

CME Bitcoin open interest at 6-month low ($25B, down from $42B in May). This is a full leveraged-long washout. Historically, CME OI at 6-month lows entering an FOMC with a neutral-hold outcome precede a 5–15% squeeze within 2 weeks. The put-OI > call-OI institutional hedge structure means any upside resolution triggers a mechanical unwind toward calls.

XRP whale + ETF alignment: 190M XRP bought on-chain in 7 days while 6-week ETF inflow streak brings cumulative to $1.44B. Exchange reserves at 2026 low. The last time both signals aligned simultaneously in 2024, XRP ran 40% within 3 weeks. This is not a casual crowd — this is smart money + institutions building before a known legislative catalyst (CLARITY Act floor vote).

CLARITY Act floor vote window: ~8 weeks remain before Senate summer recess. Leadership has NOT scheduled debate yet. Senate floor time is constrained by concurrent budget + appropriations work. Each week without scheduling compresses the window. Any floor scheduling confirmation = near-term XRP catalyst; failure to schedule before recess = 3-month delay minimum.

SUI gasless stablecoin: $65B in 5 days. Context: DeFiLlama total cross-chain stablecoin volume is ~$300B/month. Sui just processed $65B in 5 days on a single protocol feature. This is not a speculative metric — it is genuine B2B payment adoption at a scale that most L1 blockchains have never seen in a week. Price at $0.783 has not priced this.

Coinbase premium index turned positive for first time in weeks on June 13 after being deeply negative at -0.15% through early June. Positive CB premium = US institutional demand exceeding offshore prices. This is the first clean reversal signal from US spot buyers in this correction cycle. Typically precedes a 5–10% BTC move within 5 trading days.

T. Rowe Price active crypto ETF approved June 12 — includes SUI, BTC, others in a multi-asset actively managed product. This is a structural shift: prior ETFs were single-asset (BTC or ETH). A major AM with $1.5T AUM now has a mandate to hold and rebalance altcoins including SUI. This creates recurring institutional buy pressure on rebalancing cycles that doesn't exist today.
